seoTitle: East Fremantle vs East Munglinup — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: East Fremantle vs East Munglinup: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in East Fremantle and East Munglinup.
heading: East Fremantle vs East Munglinup — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between East Fremantle, Western Australia and East Munglinup, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: East Fremantle has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to East Munglinup (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is East Fremantle or East Munglinup safer from flooding?
faq.answer: East Fremantle has a flood risk score of 20/100, while East Munglinup scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: East Fremantle scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. East Munglinup scores 34/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in East Fremantle or East Munglinup?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. East Fremantle has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
